Canopied Tomb of Sir Robert Wingfield and Elizabeth Goushill in St. Mary Church in Leatheringham. Tomb was destroyed in a period of ignorance and neglect during the 17th Century.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth FitzAlan於1366年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，她的父親Sir Richard FitzAlan 11th Earl of Arundel20歲，母親Elizabeth de Bohun16歲。她於大約1378年與Sir William de Montagu在英國素可阿盧德爾結婚。她去世於1425年7月8日在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾霍福林加姆享年59歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾霍福林加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth FitzAlan于1366年出生在英国素可阿盧德爾时，她的父亲Sir Richard FitzAlan 11th Earl of Arundel20岁，母亲Elizabeth de Bohun16岁。她于大约1378年与Sir William de Montagu在英国素可阿盧德爾结婚。她去世于1425年7月8日在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾霍福林加姆享年59岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾霍福林加姆。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth FitzAlan was born in 1366, in Arundel, Sussex, England, her father, Sir Richard FitzAlan 11th Earl of Arundel, was 20 and her mother, Elizabeth de Bohun, was 16. She married Sir William de Montagu about 1378, in Arundel, Sussex, England. She died on 8 July 1425, in Hoveringham, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59, and was buried in Hoveringham, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Sir William de Montagu environ 1378, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 8 juillet 1425, à Hoveringham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterrée à Hoveringham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth FitzAlan(이)가 1366년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Richard FitzAlan 11th Earl of Arundel(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Bohun(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1378년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 Sir William de Montagu(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1425년 7월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 호버링함에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 호버링함에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth FitzAlan родилась в 1366 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Richard FitzAlan 11th Earl of Arundel было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Bohun, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Montagu около 1378 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия. Она умерла 8 июля 1425 г. в местности ховирингхам, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности ховирингхам, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.FROM 2 JUL 1403 TO 21 JUL 1404: desde 2 de julio de 1403 hasta 21 de julio de 1404
es.Saint Mary Church, Reddish, Stockport, G: Saint Mary Church, Reddish, Greater Manchester, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth FitzAlan nació en 1366, en Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard FitzAlan 11th Earl of Arundel, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Bohun, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Sir William de Montagu aproximadamente 1378, en Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra. Murió el 8 de julio de 1425, en Hoveringham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultada en Hoveringham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
